title: Consumer Lifecycle, Cancellation, Readiness, and Graceful Shutdown · Type-System Trace: Consume Registration Readiness
summary: A consumer reports ready before consume-ok, ignores broker cancellation, closes the connection while handlers run, and assumes cancel immediately stops every in-flight delivery. Planned artifact: Add and verify the consume registration readiness increment in consumer registration, readiness, and shutdown state machine. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Report ready only after connection, topology, channel, prefetch, consume registration, and required dependencies are ready.
Retire confirmed entries, reject nacked entries, preserve ambiguous entries, cancel waiters, and prove zero memory or callback leaks across channel generations.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and TypeScript Predict Consume Registration Readiness · Theory Consume Registration Readin…
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory messaging team handles case 9497 in a partner publisher using an unroutable changed topic key. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to sequence readiness, cancellation, in-flight draining, deadline, and channel close; keep every queue and subscriber responsibility explicitly named; then close the channel while one acknowledgement decision is still pending. Browser code models pure messaging decisions only. amqplib, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, external effects, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Report ready only after connection, topology, channel, prefetch, consume registration, and required dependencies are ready.
